text.title: A/B Testing Basics Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Primary metric did not move; team announces 'we actually meant clicks, not conversions'.
antipattern.fix: Lock the primary metric in a pre-registration file checked into git on day 0.

antipattern.symptom: Daily Slack updates declare significance after 3 days; experiment shipped early.
antipattern.fix: Hide significance until the target sample size is reached, or use a sequential testing framework with documented alpha-spending.

antipattern.symptom: Same user sees control on Monday and treatment on Tuesday; conversion attribution garbled.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used random() per request instead of deterministic hashing.
antipattern.fix: Switch to hash(experiment_id+user_id)%10000 bucketing; pin a unit test asserting stability.
